description: Step-level routing handlers (onPass/onFail/onWarning/onSkip + continue/stop). Every permutation here resolves PASS or SKIPPED so it lands in the combined no-fail suite; the FAIL-path behaviors (onFail:continue running past a failure, the default onFail:stop) are asserted in focused it() tests in core-core.test.js. Cross-platform: uses `node -e` runShell so it resolves on every OS.

tests.description: A handler that is present but not triggered is a no-op: onFail:[{continue:true}] on a PASSING step never fires, so the step PASSes exactly as if no handler were set (additive/byte-identical).

steps.description: Passes; the onFail handler is never reached.

tests.description: onPass:[{stop:'test'}] halts the test after a passing step: step 1 PASSes and stops, so step 2 is SKIPPED. With a PASS present the test rolls up PASS.

onSkip:[{stop:'test'}] on a reached-but-skipped step halts the rest: step 1 is guard-`if` false -> SKIPPED, its onSkip routes stop, so step 2 is SKIPPED too. All steps SKIPPED -> the test rolls up SKIPPED.

steps.description: Guard false -> SKIPPED; onSkip routes stop.

tests.description: onWarning:[{continue:true}] on a passing step is a no-op (no warning is produced): the step PASSes.

A routing entry whose `if` selector does not match is skipped, falling back to the status default. onPass:[{if:'$$platform == nonexistentos', stop:'test'}] never matches, so the default (continue) applies and step 2 still runs -> PASS.

steps.description: Passes; the stop entry's `if` never matches, so default continue applies.
